title: Chapter Eleven: The Challenge
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The brick tower stood twelve spans high.

Yeon-shin studied it from ten paces, reading the joints, the mortar lines, the subtle lean where the foundation had settled unevenly into the packed earth. He held the Desolate sword at his side, sheathed, the hilt warm in his hand.

*Force. Environment. Target.*

He breathed. The true energy stirred along his meridians -- outward, not inward, flowing through his limbs and into the air around the blade. Radiant Demon Formation. The world simplified. Lines of force became visible, not to his eyes but to something deeper. A transparent map drawn in the space between intention and action.

The sword was already moving.

He did not draw on internal energy to boost his strength. He simply followed where the qi led -- a single, unhurried arc. The muscles in his chest fired first, force travelling through deltoid and biceps to the deep flexor tendons of his fingers. One path. One moment.

Every joint in his body locked.

*Crack.* The tower collapsed. Bricks spilled across the training ground in a cascade of dust and rubble, settling in a heap that looked remarkably similar to the one Ma Jin had destroyed beside it.

*Radiant Demon Formation. Mastered.*

He suppressed the excitement that surged in his chest. Sheathed the sword at his waist. Turned.

Ma Jin was staring at him with an expression more intense than the one he had worn while duelling Cheong Myeong.

"What... is this."

"I need real combat," Yeon-shin said. "To use Radiant Demon Formation in a split-second life-or-death moment -- ordinary training will not be enough."

Silence.

"I understand what it means to engrave it in the body," Yeon-shin added. He clasped his fists in salute. "I have learned well, Chief."

Ma Jin, said to be among the seventeen strongest warriors in the Divine Sword Unit, stood for a long time without speaking.

---

In a tavern in the Xiangyang district, a dozen Radiant Demon Wing masters sat drinking.

"That boy," one of them said. "He is strange."

Blue martial uniforms, every one of them. The character for *wild* embroidered at both shoulders and back. Cups raised, faces flushed with drink.

"I have heard that said a great deal," another man replied. "Until I came here."

"A once-in-a-generation talent? That is nothing new."

"How many martial artists in Desolate Sect were not called talented when they first arrived?"

A faint scent of alcohol clung to every word. No one bothered to circulate their inner power to burn the drink from their blood. Those who lived as ascetics did not come to taverns.

"That is what I am saying," the first man continued. "That is what ordinary folk say about geniuses. 'He is strange.' But to us -- to the masters of Radiant Demon Wing -- this boy is strange. Has anyone seen the Chief make that face?"

"I heard he is already using Radiant Demon Formation with skill."

"Watch him swing a sword. He has form now. When he fought Cheong Myeong, he was like a young beast raised without parents. Now he is something else."

A younger-faced warrior leaned forward. "I like the Lightning Flash boy's temperament. He is not arrogant. With that talent, he could be."

"Sometimes he is so brazen it is bewildering. We will know his true nature after his first real mission."

"If there are no issues, he will be the first name anyone mentions when they speak of Desolate Sect in the future. Would he not soon rise from white to blue?"

"That is possible."

"If he survives."

Several masters nodded. In the Desolate Sect, talent was a beginning, not an end. The graveyard behind the eastern compound held more prodigies than the living quarters.

"With that level of talent, the Sect Lord would have taken notice," someone said. "She would have seen through him at a glance at the last Desolate ceremony. There may be some arrangement."

Nods around the table.

"But what those fools next door are saying --" A warrior turned his head toward the wall. "That is ridiculous."

The woman beside him -- cold eyes, sharp features, aura already raised -- grinned.

"They are upset," she said. "They made a great fuss about wanting Lightning Flash or White Qilin for their captain, and in the end they could not get either one."

Laughter.

The wall exploded.

Brick and timber blew inward. The Radiant Demon Wing masters deflected the shrapnel with chopsticks and wine cups, casual gestures that contained profound martial principles.

Through the dust came warriors in the same blue uniform. Azure Sky Squad. One man stepped forward, his robes fluttering with fully raised qi.

"Sorry to interrupt," he said. His tone said he was not sorry at all.

The woman who had been grinning rose to her feet, her aura sharpening like a drawn blade.

"If you knew that, you would have left quietly. We were just getting in the mood." Her smile was a provocation. "Our youngest has amazing talent."

"Bewitching Sword Demon. Are you looking for a fight?"

"I thought your Azure Sky Squad wanted one first. Saying talent has clear limits in Radiant Demon Wing? Spewing insults you knew we could hear?"

Her tone hardened with each word. Her aura intensified to match. She stood and glared.

"Draw your swords. Let me see your eighteen moves."

The Xiangyang Best Tavern descended into chaos.

---

The next morning, Yeon-shin walked to the training ground.

"It is our youngest!"

"There is no lesson today, yet you came to train?"

"An admirable attitude."

He returned the seniors' greetings with a fist-and-palm salute, then walked straight to Ma Jin. The Chief's face was haggard -- not from physical fatigue, but from thought. He had spent the night working out how to teach the unteachable.

"Is there something you are stuck on?"

"I would like to have a duel," Yeon-shin said.

"Very well. Pick anyone. They will all oblige."

"Not with Radiant Demon Wing." Yeon-shin kept his voice level. "I want to compete against Azure Sky Squad."

Silence.

Every warrior on the training ground knew about the tavern.

"You must have heard about yesterday's incident," Ma Jin said carefully. "There is no need to get worked up. Calm yourself."

*I am not worked up.*

Ma Jin's face showed bewilderment and pride in equal measure.

"What do you mean? I simply wish to witness the Azure Sky sword technique that I have long admired."

"You are not calming down at all."

But behind him, the seniors' faces told a different story. Many of them were beaming. Battle-hardened warriors, no less skilled than the masters of the Nine Sects, their eyes flashing like drawn steel.

*I intended to earn their favour. That was faster than expected.*

"I want to test myself against their renowned sword technique using Radiant Demon Formation," Yeon-shin said. "Please allow it, Chief."

*Real combat experience. A chance to study an advanced sword art. The seniors' respect.*

Three birds. One stone. The last one was already his.

"Even under the umbrella of Desolate Sect's Divine Sword Unit, Radiant Demon Wing is different," Ma Jin warned. "They will not go easy on you."

"That is what I am hoping for."

Whistles. Cheers.

Ma Jin nodded.

"Very well. I will inform the Azure Sky Squad Leader."

The next day, a duel was arranged. Radiant Demon Wing's Lightning Flash against a blue-rank warrior of the Azure Sky Squad.

They called it an exchange match. The entire Desolate Sect called it something else.

A reckoning.

---
